title: Medical ingest: lab-PDF -> vault notes + profile + supplements
description: Adds the lab-ingestion leg of the medical knowledge template: a lab-report PDF is parsed (Claude `document` block, no OCR) into structured markers, written as markdown-with-frontmatter notes in the canonical vault, and reindexed into the existing HealthDataStore as the derived index. Also adds a markdown-frontmatter supplements list flattened into FactStore and a SOPS-encrypted personalization profile.yaml (age/sex/conditions/meds/supplements/allergies/goals).

features.title: Lab-PDF structured parser (egress-gated)
features.description: Parse a native-text lab-report PDF into a Zod-validated ParsedLabReport (panel, collection date, markers each with name/value/unit/reference range) using a Claude `document` content block via an injectable LLMClient. Cloud parse is honoured only behind the cloud-inference egress axis; otherwise the call is refused fail-closed.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: parseLabPdf base64-encodes a PDF and sends it as an Anthropic `document` content block through an injectable LLMClient, returning a Zod-validated ParsedLabReport.
AC2: Malformed model output (missing required marker fields) is rejected by Zod and never returned as an unvalidated object.
AC3: The provider layer gains an additive optional ChatParams.documents field rendered only by the Anthropic adapter; other adapters ignore it and existing calls remain byte-identical.

features.title: Lab-note vault storage + derived index + ingest dedup
features.description: Serialize each parsed marker to a markdown note with structured YAML frontmatter (marker/value/unit/ref_low/ref_high/ref_range/date/status/panel/source) under the canonical vault directory, and reindex those notes into the existing HealthDataStore.lab_results. Status is derived in deterministic JS; duplicates dedup via the deterministic labResultId under INSERT OR IGNORE.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: writeLabNote writes a markdown file whose frontmatter carries the full marker schema and is parseable back into the same fields.
AC2: reindexLabNotes parses a vault directory of lab notes and upserts them into HealthDataStore.lab_results so getLabSeries(marker) returns each result.
AC3: Re-running reindex over unchanged notes inserts zero new rows (deterministic-id dedup).
AC4: Lab status (normal/low/high) is computed in deterministic JS from value vs reference range, not by an LLM.

features.title: bober medical import-labs <pdf> end-to-end command
features.description: Wire the parser (feat-1) and vault writer + reindex (feat-2) into a new `bober medical import-labs <pdf>` subcommand. The command refuses fail-closed when the cloud-inference egress axis is off, dedups at ingest, and appends an IDs/enums-only audit entry.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: With cloud-inference enabled and an injected fake parser, the command writes a lab note, reindexes so getLabSeries returns the marker, and appends an audit `ingest` entry.
AC2: With cloud-inference off (default), the command prints a clear message, sets exit code 1, and never invokes the parser or reads the PDF.
AC3: Re-importing the same report adds zero new lab_results rows (ingest-time dedup).

features.title: Supplements markdown-frontmatter list -> FactStore
features.description: A markdown-frontmatter supplements list flattened into FactStore via reconcile (dedup-aware), plus `bober medical supplements add|list` subcommands.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: `supplements add` reconciles a supplement (name + dose) into FactStore under the medical scope; getActiveFacts returns it.
AC2: Re-adding the same supplement+dose is a NOOP via reconcileFact exact-match (active count stays 1).
AC3: `supplements list` reads the supplements markdown-frontmatter file and prints each name + dose.

features.title: Personalization profile.yaml (SOPS-encrypted)
features.description: A SOPS-encrypted personalization profile (age/sex/conditions/medications/supplements/allergies/goals) with Zod validation and an injectable cipher seam, plus `bober medical profile show|set` subcommands.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: writeProfile serializes to YAML, encrypts via the cipher seam, and writes <vaultDir>/profile.yaml; readProfile decrypts and returns a ProfileSchema-validated object that round-trips.
AC2: When the cipher seam reports SOPS unavailable, read/write refuse with a clear message and write no plaintext profile to disk.
AC3: ProfileSchema rejects invalid input (negative age, unknown sex).

assumptions: DESIGN-APPROVAL (gate): autonomous self-approval. Every assumption below cites file:line evidence in the agent-bober substrate and the locked research doc; approval evidence recorded in metadata.approvalEvidence.

ChatParams gains an additive optional `documents` field rendered only by the Anthropic adapter (other adapters ignore it) -- evidence: src/providers/types.ts ChatParams optional-field pattern (responseSchema/jsonObjectMode) and src/providers/anthropic.ts toAnthropicMessage content-block builder (lines 80-132).

Claude parses native-text lab PDFs to structured JSON without OCR; scanned/image PDFs are out of scope -- evidence: research-20260627-knowledge-platform-landscape.md section 2 Angle 3 (line 67) and section 4 (line 165).

When the cloud-inference egress axis is off (default), import-labs fails closed without reading the PDF or opening a connection, mirroring the WHOOP device-connection gate -- evidence: src/cli/commands/medical.ts:54-62 and the fail-closed resolver src/medical/inference.ts:43-49.

Parsed markers map onto the existing LabResult shape and dedup via the deterministic labResultId -- evidence: src/medical/types.ts:118-127 (LabResult) and src/medical/health-store.ts:48-53,212-230 (labResultId, upsertLabResult INSERT OR IGNORE).
The vault is canonical and the SQLite store is a derived, rebuildable index; reconcile/dedup runs at ingest time -- evidence: research section 3b (lines 150-153).

This spec does NOT hard-import the sibling vault-store module (spec-20260628-obsidian-vault-store); the medical lab-note writer and reindexer write/parse plain markdown+frontmatter directly so the build stays green regardless of sibling timing -- evidence: orchestrator dependsOn directive + research section 3b vault-canonical rule.

Lab status (normal/low/high) is computed in deterministic JS from value vs reference range; only a source-PDF critical flag is carried through -- evidence: deterministic-numerics guarantee in src/medical/numerics.ts and research section 4a #5 (line 183).

Supplements flatten into FactStore via reconcileFact/writeFact (exact-match NOOP/UPDATE dedup) -- evidence: src/orchestrator/memory/reconcile.ts:51-101,148-154 and src/state/facts.ts FactStore (line 136).

SOPS encrypts only the small structured profile.yaml (age backend, local, no egress); a real sops binary is abstracted behind an injectable cipher seam for testability -- evidence: research section 3b (lines 155-156) and the injectable-factory pattern at src/medical/inference.ts:17 + src/cli/commands/medical.ts:31-36 (WhoopSyncDeps).
outOfScope: Analysis/insights, proactive review pass, and the recommendation judge-loop (owned by spec-20260628-medical-analysis).
The generic vault layer + generic reindex + Dataview dashboards (owned by spec-20260628-obsidian-vault-store).
The unified priority hub and task inbox.
Rebuilding Apple Health (SAX) or WHOOP importers -- they are reused as-is.
Syncthing / binary-attachment handling for scan PDFs.

clarificationQuestions.question: How should a lab PDF reach the model, given the LLMClient abstraction has no document/PDF content block today?

options.label: A
options.description: Add an additive optional ChatParams.documents field rendered only by the Anthropic adapter; other adapters ignore it.

options.label: B
options.description: Bypass the LLMClient seam and call the Anthropic SDK directly inside the parser.

clarificationQuestions.recommendation: A -- keeps the provider seam intact and additive; evidence src/providers/anthropic.ts:80-132.

clarificationQuestions.question: When cloud-inference egress is off (default) and local llama3 cannot parse a PDF, what should import-labs do?

options.label: A
options.description: Fail closed: print a clear message, exit 1, never read the PDF or open a connection (mirror the WHOOP gate).

options.label: B
options.description: Attempt a local parse and degrade silently.

clarificationQuestions.recommendation: A -- mirrors src/cli/commands/medical.ts:54-62 and inference fail-closed at src/medical/inference.ts:43-49.

clarificationQuestions.question: What is the canonical lab-note frontmatter schema and how does it map to the existing LabResult store?

options.label: A
options.description: frontmatter marker/value/unit/ref_low/ref_high/ref_range/date/status/panel/source; reindex maps to LabResult via upsertLabResult.

clarificationQuestions.recommendation: A -- maps onto src/medical/types.ts:118-127 and src/medical/health-store.ts:212-230.

clarificationQuestions.question: Does this spec depend on the sibling vault-store module's exported API at build time?

options.label: A
options.description: No hard import: write/parse plain markdown+frontmatter directly so the build is independent of sibling timing.

options.label: B
options.description: Import the sibling module and block the build until it lands.

clarificationQuestions.recommendation: A -- vault-canonical/derived-index rule, research section 3b lines 150-153.

clarificationQuestions.question: How is SOPS encryption of profile.yaml made testable without a real sops binary in CI?

options.label: A
options.description: Inject a cipher seam (encrypt/decrypt) defaulting to a sops age shell-out; tests inject a reversible fake; missing sops -> refuse.

clarificationQuestions.recommendation: A -- injectable-factory pattern src/medical/inference.ts:17 and src/cli/commands/medical.ts:31-36.

resolvedClarifications.answer: Autonomous self-approval. Plan implements one locked part (medical lab ingestion) of the approved research design; every assumption cites file:line evidence in agent-bober + research-20260627-knowledge-platform-landscape.md.

A -- add an additive optional ChatParams.documents field rendered only by the Anthropic adapter (others ignore). Evidence: src/providers/types.ts ChatParams optional fields; src/providers/anthropic.ts:80-132.
A -- fail closed (message + exit 1, no PDF read, no connection). Evidence: src/cli/commands/medical.ts:54-62; src/medical/inference.ts:43-49.

A -- frontmatter marker/value/unit/ref_low/ref_high/ref_range/date/status/panel/source mapped to LabResult via upsertLabResult. Evidence: src/medical/types.ts:118-127; src/medical/health-store.ts:212-230.
A -- no hard import of the sibling vault-store module; write/parse plain markdown+frontmatter directly. Evidence: research section 3b lines 150-153.
A -- injectable cipher seam defaulting to sops age shell-out; reversible fake in tests; missing sops -> refuse. Evidence: src/medical/inference.ts:17; src/cli/commands/medical.ts:31-36.

techStack: SOPS (age backend, abstracted behind an injectable cipher seam)

techNotes.dataModel: Vault is canonical (markdown+frontmatter). Derived indexes: lab results -> HealthDataStore.lab_results (deterministic labResultId dedup); supplements + profile meds -> FactStore (reconcile dedup). ParsedLabReport { panel, collectedAtIso, markers[] } where each marker -> LabResult { biomarker, value, unit, collectedAtIso, referenceLow?, referenceHigh? }.
integrationPoints: Anthropic provider adapter -- new document content block for native-PDF parse (cloud, gated).
Existing EgressGuard cloud-inference axis (src/medical/egress.ts) -- gates the cloud PDF parse.
securityConsiderations: Zero-egress default preserved: cloud PDF parse only when medical.egress.cloudInference is true; otherwise fail closed before reading bytes.
Deterministic JS lab-status classification (no LLM numeric judgement) preserves the existing deterministic-numerics guarantee.
Audit log records IDs/enums only -- never health values or prompt text.
profile.yaml SOPS-encrypted (age, local, no network); missing sops refuses rather than writing plaintext PHI.
Lab numbers are import/command-only -- humans never hand-edit numeric frontmatter (research section 3b line 153).

nonFunctionalRequirements.requirement: No outbound network bytes for lab parsing unless medical.egress.cloudInference is true; the off path must exit before reading the PDF.
nonFunctionalRequirements.verificationMethod: unit-test asserting the injected parser/client is never invoked and no file is read when the axis is off

nonFunctionalRequirements.requirement: Re-ingesting an identical lab report or supplement produces zero new store rows (idempotent ingest).
nonFunctionalRequirements.verificationMethod: unit-test asserting second-run newRows is 0 and active fact count is unchanged

nonFunctionalRequirements.requirement: The medical ingest modules do not hard-import the sibling vault-store module, keeping the build independent of sibling timing.
nonFunctionalRequirements.verificationMethod: build (tsc) succeeds with no reference to the sibling module

constraints: Additive only -- existing medical/provider/store callers stay byte-identical when the new fields/commands are unused.
All timestamps injected (no Date.now() in store/pure modules) per the existing medical purity contract.
